tweets.text:
The bottom is in!

Well, sorta.

It’s actually still forming, and according to NUPL (Net Unrealized Profit/Loss), Bitcoin is currently sitting around 0.158.

That puts it inside the fear zone, meaning most of the profit from the bull market has already been erased. Feelgoodman.

However, the market still hasn’t reached full capitulation.

NUPL is still above zero, while the 30-day NUPL EMA is sitting around 0.155 and the 100-day EMA is closer to 0.215.

The shorter average crossed below the longer one on June 2, which basically shows that holder profitability and market momentum are still weakening.

So yeah, Bitcoin holders are hurting, but we still haven’t reached the same level of pain seen during previous full bear-market washouts.

I personally think $58K could’ve been the bottom, but historically, another capitulation move wouldn’t be unusual, and I’ve got my hopes up right now.

Either way, I think the best thing to do is stay realistic and stay safe.
tweets.full_text:
The bottom is in!

Well, sorta.

It’s actually still forming, and according to NUPL (Net Unrealized Profit/Loss), Bitcoin is currently sitting around 0.158.

That puts it inside the fear zone, meaning most of the profit from the bull market has already been erased. Feelgoodman.

However, the market still hasn’t reached full capitulation.

NUPL is still above zero, while the 30-day NUPL EMA is sitting around 0.155 and the 100-day EMA is closer to 0.215.

The shorter average crossed below the longer one on June 2, which basically shows that holder profitability and market momentum are still weakening.

So yeah, Bitcoin holders are hurting, but we still haven’t reached the same level of pain seen during previous full bear-market washouts.

I personally think $58K could’ve been the bottom, but historically, another capitulation move wouldn’t be unusual, and I’ve got my hopes up right now.

Either way, I think the best thing to do is stay realistic and stay safe.

Latest news on Hormuz linked below. If agreed, #oil will be down on Monday while #silver, #gold and #uranium will do very well. But I doubt things will be smooth.

CNN says that Oman has proposed splitting Strait of Hormuz shipping into two lanes: A southern route through Omani waters with normal free passage, and a northern route through Iranian waters that would require Iran's prior approval but no transit fees. The proposal is still being negotiated, and Iranian and Omani officials discussed it in Muscat.

tweets.text:
To me almost all of the doc feels fully compatible with worlds where the final "turn over control to ASI in 2040" step actually means "12 people simultaneously release the trusted hardware keys that unlock the next generation of super-GPUs, which leads to a phase change of AI strength resulting in lots of different ASIs simultaneously turning on that serve different groups", or even worlds where you avoid that kind of step entirely, and instead, we stay "in the centaur zone" for a decade or a few and that period only ends once uploading becomes available and safe, and at that point if you choose you can upload yourself and become competitive with ASIs.

Both of those "epilogues" are multipolar, and hence incompatible with a permanently-vulnerable world where black balls at the technological ceiling exist. But the steps needed to get there, if you share all of AI 2040's underlying assumptions, are basically the same.

(CC @DKokotajlo )

(CC @DKokotajlo )

thread_tweets.text:
I agree, I'm not assuming there's a single-dimensional "intelligence" variable that decides everything, my model is more that humans have a whole range of capabilities, machines are already superhuman at maybe 80% of them (if that number sounds high, think: how would a peasant in 1500 spend their day, and how much of that is now automated?), and the big question is whether LLMs or very-near-future extensions of them swallow up the entire remaining 20% or whether there's some 5% left (which could include what we call creativity)

In the latter case, that 5% will become the only scarce thing, and so we'll notice and think about it a lot, the economy will organize around it, it will be the primary source of wages, etc.

tweets.text:
To me the strongest argument for delaying AI having very high humanitarian costs in a normal-tech world goes through the hypothesis that curing aging and globally providing extremely cheap universally available clean food, water and medicine may both be possible when and only when we get much better science and manufacturing enabled by very powerful AI.

And the most compelling counterargument to that that I've seen is that at some point the most important blockers will not be intelligence, but rather things like physical limits to speed of real-world deployment, the loop of waiting for results from real-world experiments and adapting in response, etc. And so we may get close to maximum speed even with just today's (or today + 1-2 years) AI. Also, pauses would focus on frontier model improvements, not on R&D in deploying existing AI into our production and science processes, so we'll get lots of speedup from there too regardless of AI progress rates.

As for "plan A bastardization risk", I wrote one version of that here: https://t.co/sz7yDdhSxT
tweets.full_text:
To me the strongest argument for delaying AI having very high humanitarian costs in a normal-tech world goes through the hypothesis that curing aging and globally providing extremely cheap universally available clean food, water and medicine may both be possible when and only when we get much better science and manufacturing enabled by very powerful AI.

And the most compelling counterargument to that that I've seen is that at some point the most important blockers will not be intelligence, but rather things like physical limits to speed of real-world deployment, the loop of waiting for results from real-world experiments and adapting in response, etc. And so we may get close to maximum speed even with just today's (or today + 1-2 years) AI. Also, pauses would focus on frontier model improvements, not on R&D in deploying existing AI into our production and science processes, so we'll get lots of speedup from there too regardless of AI progress rates.

As for "plan A bastardization risk", I wrote one version of that here: https://t.co/sz7yDdhSxT

tweets.text:
Update 3: Over the past 48 hours an entity initially funded by the LAB team deposited 18.4M LAB ($18.3M) to Aster and began selling LAB spot on the DEX which has caused the LAB price to drop another -54% from $1.2 to $0.55.

From July 10 to 11 the entity began transferring LAB to three Aster deposit addresses and currently still holds another 81.5M LAB:
0xaad30cab22f772c1658b7845b5837d35bf3a467a
0x76ccfde9819500204985580d235dd8326fa0b241
0x628dd74f428a81cd34ece11331a7f1593f76047a

The entity received 196M+ LAB from the LAB team in April 2026, transferred LAB to four Bitget deposit addresses, withdrew to ten addresses from May 11 to 12, and still holds 81.5M LAB.

tweets.text:
Reduce size
Plan your entries instead of fomoing
Take profit quickly instead of being greedy
Ignore setups when you can't get in in a position of strength
Don't try to force quick profits

Following these risk mgmt rules will minimize your drawdowns and make your life easier

thread_tweets.text: @yasu0x1 So many of the mistakes I've made in trading were downstream of either fomo'ing in bad r/r spot or just irrationally sizing up in a position of weakness
thread_tweets.full_text: @yasu0x1 So many of the mistakes I've made in trading were downstream of either fomo'ing in bad r/r spot or just irrationally sizing up in a position of weakness

thread_tweets.text: @DeFimorphosis Very few non memecoins have been in an up only state. On example is JTO: you were almost always better off TPing on its squeezes and then re-assessing instead of trying to hold from bottom to top
thread_tweets.full_text: @DeFimorphosis Very few non memecoins have been in an up only state. On example is JTO: you were almost always better off TPing on its squeezes and then re-assessing instead of trying to hold from bottom to top

tweets.full_text:
Reduce size
Plan your entries instead of fomoing
Take profit quickly instead of being greedy
Ignore setups when you can't get in in a position of strength
Don't try to force quick profits

Following these risk mgmt rules will minimize your drawdowns and make your life easier

@yasu0x1 So many of the mistakes I've made in trading were downstream of either fomo'ing in bad r/r spot or just irrationally sizing up in a position of weakness

@DeFimorphosis Very few non memecoins have been in an up only state. On example is JTO: you were almost always better off TPing on its squeezes and then re-assessing instead of trying to hold from bottom to top
